summary: A legendary second electric set (Casey Jones, Good Lovin', Viola Lee Blues) shines on a mostly solid but uneven sound‑board recording.
review: The show is praised as one of the great sets of ’70 with a bang‑starting Casey Jones, a scorching Good Lovin'/NFA/drums sandwich and standout performances of Viola Lee Blues, High Times and Lovelight. The recording is a soundboard source that sounds excellent in places but suffers from low‑quality sections and mic/level changes early in the set.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is consistently high (average ~4.3) while recording quality varies widely (average ~2.5); weighted combination yields a 3.7 rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: First five tracks are low‑fidelity AUD recordings; later electric set is clear. Mic swaps and level changes cause occasional muddiness, but overall the soundboard captures the atmosphere well.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong opening with Casey Jones, smooth transition into acoustic material, then an electric second set that “smokes” with tight jams and seamless segues.
